name: Frank Socolow
abstract: Frank Socolow (September 18, 1923 – April 30, 1981), born in New York City, was a jazz saxophonist and oboist, noted for his tenor playing. Socolow began his career in the early 1940s playing in swing bands led by Jack Nelson, Georgie Auld, Ted Fio Rito, Roy Stevens, Van Alexander and Shep Fields.

about_quotes.speaker: George Handy
about_quotes.evidence: He worked with me this past summer. I used him in my summer band

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Gibbs
about_quotes.evidence: We went to Sweden with that group. Conte Candoli, Lou Levy and Frank Socolow on tenor and Denzel Best on drums.
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Gibbs interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2001 [interview 1 of 2]
